subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Aim statement clearly identifies the purpose and outlines statistical methods
Use of headings, tables and graphs gives the exploration an obvious structure
weaknesses: Explanations are verbose and contain redundant personal anecdotes
Transitions between sections are sometimes abrupt and the narrative doubles back
Some tables span pages without captions or clear reference, reducing flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission shows adequate organisation and coherence overall (Band 2). The sequence of sections and clear aim statement give readers a solid structure. However, verbosity and redundant passages weaken conciseness, and abrupt transitions plus uncaptioned tables disrupt the flow. This aligns with a middle-band performance (2/4).

strengths: Uses appropriate symbols (r, RÂ², Î¼, Ï, ÏÂ²) and presents key formulae
Includes multiple representations (scatter plots, frequency tables, court diagram) to support the argument
Explanations generally match symbols and graphs, allowing the argument to be followed
weaknesses: Typographical/notation inconsistencies (e.g., misâcopied numbers, mixed subscripts, LaTeX errors)
Several axes and units are missing on plots and diagrams
Notation is not consistently accurate throughout, reducing clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is mostly clear but inconsistent in notation and labelling (Band 2). While formulae and representations are present, frequent typographical errors and missing axis labels hinder consistency. The work fits the descriptors for a mid-band performance (2/4).

strengths: Some initiative shown by exploring alternative correlation methods and widening chi-squared sample
Personal voice appears in reflections (e.g., âas a shooter myselfâ)
Choice of methods shows limited but genuine personal interest
weaknesses: Independent thinking is limited; methods closely follow textbook examples
Investigation is not driven by a distinctive personal strategy
Conclusions largely restate numerical results without deeper insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The personal engagement is present but only at a basic level (Band 1). Although personal voice and choice of alternative tests show some initiative, the work does not demonstrate significant independent thinking or a highly personal approach. Thus a score of 1/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Reflects on what statistical results mean and links them to the research question
Attempts a concluding synthesis of findings
weaknesses: Reflection remains surface-level with minimal probing of assumptions
Critical analysis is basic, lacking depth on methodology or sample justification
Connections to enhanced understanding are brief and occasionally contradictory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is evident but limited in depth and critical rigour (Band 1). The student comments on results but does not extensively critique methodology or explore underlying assumptions, fitting a low-band profile. A score of 1/3 reflects this performance.

strengths: Employs relevant HL course techniques (correlation coefficients, t-tests, chi-squared, projectile motion)
Chi-squared test explanation is well contextualised and links observed vs expected frequencies
Insightful comparative discussion of Pearson vs Spearman methods
weaknesses: Several transcription and rounding errors (e.g., vÂ² calculation typo) affect accuracy
Omissions in degrees of freedom, p-value interpretation and significance level consistency
Some class interval definitions and unit specifications are unclear
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The use of mathematics is solidly at course level with sound understanding (Band 3), demonstrating relevant techniques and contextual insights. However, partial accuracy issues and missing interpretative details prevent a higher band. A mid-range score of 3/6 accurately reflects performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: The choice to test alternative correlations and to widen the chi-squared sample shows some initiative, but conclusions often merely restate numerical outcomes without deeper insight, and the work follows textbook procedures closely.  Independent thinking is therefore present only in a limited way.

The student demonstrates sound understanding of correlation, hypothesis testing and basic projectile motion, though explanations sometimes blur statistical vs causal inference.  Understanding is good in parts but not fully sophisticated, aligning with band 3.

Most computations appear numerically reasonable, yet several transcription/rounding errors (e.g., 5.14.2, incorrect Spearman interpretation vs p-value threshold) show partial accuracy.  Mathematics is therefore partially correct, fitting band 2.

Most content is relevant to the research question, yet explanations are verbose and several paragraphs restate basketball context or personal opinions already given.  Redundancy around "as a shooter myself" and duplicated descriptions of Pearson vs Spearman show the work is not consistently concise.

Key symbols (r, RÂ²,  Î¼, Ï, ÏÂ²) are used and formulae are provided, but LaTeX rendering errors (e.g., $\overline{v}$, mixed subscripts) and unit omissions appear.  Notation is therefore appropriate in many places yet lacks the consistency and accuracy required for higher bands.

Explanations generally match the symbols/graphs and the reader can follow the argument, yet sporadic typographical mistakes, mis-copied numbers (e.g., 5.14.2) and abrupt sentence breaks cause inconsistency.  Hence clarity is mostly, but not fully, achieved.

Correlation coefficients, t-tests, chi-squared and projectile equations are all at HL course level and relevant to the aim, though not extended beyond syllabus.  Hence mathematics is ârelevant at course levelâ but not yet âused effectivelyâ throughout.

Scatter plots, frequency table and court diagram support the mathematics, but axes are sometimes un-labelled (Spearman rank plots) and no scale accompanies the court sketch.  Multiple representations are present, though their quality and interpretation are mixed.

The student comments on what each statistical result âmeansâ, yet reflections are often surface-level (e.g., âthere has to be some correlationâ), with little probing of assumptions or limitations.  Reflection therefore exists but is limited.

Selecting alternative statistics (Spearman, chi-squared) shows some creativity, but overall execution mirrors common examples and does not extend into novel modelling or original data collection.  Initiative is therefore limited.

The report follows a recognisable introduction-method-results-conclusion sequence and most arguments progress in a sensible order, but transitions between sections are sometimes abrupt and the narrative occasionally doubles back (e.g., repeating why Pearson vs Spearman was chosen).  The logical thread can still be followed without major difficulty, yet it is not consistently tight or sophisticated.
Analytical statements mainly repeat test outcomes; critique of data selection, sample size or model appropriateness is minimal.  Critical analysis is thus basic and fits band 1.

Links between results and the research question are stated, but explanations of how understanding has been deepened are brief and occasionally contradictory.  Connection to understanding is therefore limited.

Headings, tables and graphs give the piece an obvious structure; however, sub-sections are uneven in length, and some tables break across pages without captioning, reducing flow.  There is a clear beginning and end, but linkages between statistical tests are only moderately sign-posted, so overall organisation is adequate rather than polished.

Personal voice appears ("as a shooter myself"), yet the investigation is not driven by a clearly individual strategy; datasets and methods are standard.  The personal element adds some flavour but does not fundamentally shape the mathematics.

annotations.comment: The court diagram lacks a scale or legend, making it difficult to interpret distances visually. Include a scale indicator for accuracy.
There is a typographical error in the algebraic manipulation: "v^2 = 5.14.2 / 4.19" should be "v^2 = 514.2 / 4.19". Correct to avoid confusion.
Class intervals in the chi-squared frequency table overlap ambiguously (e.g., ââ¤â vs â<â boundaries). Define nonâoverlapping, uniform bins.
The explanation of the chi-squared test is well contextualized, clearly linking observed and expected frequencies to the normal distribution hypothesis.
The summation of (OâE)Â²/E values to compute ÏÂ² omits reference to degrees of freedom and critical value; include these for full interpretation.
The concluding reflection effectively synthesizes findings but could engage more critically with study limitations and potential future extensions.
The aim statement clearly identifies the investigationâs purpose and outlines the statistical methods, providing a solid foundation for the exploration.
The title page lacks a concise research question phrased as a question; reformulate it to improve focus and coherence.
The introduction is very verbose, with repeated personal anecdotes and context. Trimming redundant sections will enhance conciseness and relevance.
Consider structuring the introduction into subheadings or bullet points to break up large text blocks and improve reader navigation.
The projectile motion equation is presented inline but lacks explicit unit consistency (e.g., units for g, x, y). Add units to ensure clarity.
The sampling of the top 30 three-point shooters is unmotivated. Justify why this sample and size are appropriate for statistical significance.
Table 1 spans the page without a caption or clear reference in the text. Add descriptive captions and refer to it explicitly for coherence.
The secondary tableâs purpose is unclear and seems redundant. Consider integrating its content into the main table or adding context to its interpretation.
The correlation formula uses inconsistent notation for the mean of y as â\overline{v}â instead of â\overline{y}â. Standardize symbols throughout.
The scatter plot on page 8 lacks axis labels and units, hindering interpretation. Label axes (e.g., % rank) and include units.
Interpretation of R and RÂ² is given without reference to significance tests or confidence intervals; include p-values for completeness.
The ranked data table is extensive yet precedes any summary statistic; introduce key summary (mean, median) before the full table to guide the reader.
The negative correlation between 3PT% and PPG is counterintuitive but lacks discussion of potential confounders; deepen reflection on positional/role effects.
Comparative discussion of Pearson vs Spearman methods is insightful, effectively linking mathematical choice to data characteristics.
Significance level is stated as Î± = 0.010 (1%) but later compared to p < 0.10 (10%), reflecting inconsistency. Clarify the intended Î±.
The mean Win Share calculation is clear and correctly applied, demonstrating proficiency in summarizing data.
The first table on page 16 omits the header for the index column, which reduces clarity. Add a descriptive column header.
Include a statement on the assumption of equal variances before performing the independent two-sample t-test to validate methodology.
The p-value from the t-test is reported without noting whether it is one- or two-tailed. Specify test direction for statistical rigor.
The 2023â24 Win Share table (page 16) lacks an index column and first-row labels, making cross-comparison difficult.
